problem_statement: Expected: the museum lobby display publishes approved exhibit images at 1920 pixels wide. Observed: the lobby display publishes 320-pixel preview images even though the approved source assets are high resolution. Diagnose the causal family and evidence sufficiency without publishing content or changing any kiosk settings.

observations.statement: Read-only metadata for all 24 approved exhibit assets reports widths between 2400 and 4800 pixels, while the generated lobby package contains 320-pixel outputs.

The lobby process's read-only resolved-settings trace records base display_max_width=1920 followed by a kiosk-local preview profile setting display_max_width=320; an unaffected gallery kiosk resolves only the base profile.

An isolated renderer holds binary, source assets, dependency versions, runtime image, and clock fixed; exchanging only the two captured resolved-settings snapshots swaps output width between 320 and 1920 pixels.
The lobby and gallery attest to the same imaging-library hash, and an isolated metadata decode using that library preserves each source asset's full dimensions.

A renderer rollout occurred the previous evening, but both kiosks report the same signed binary and the width disagreement persists in isolated runs of that binary under their respective settings snapshots.
